steps.title: Identify the angle pair as alternate exterior angles
steps.content: When transversal $n$ crosses parallel lines $r$ and $s$, it creates angles at each intersection. The angle $162°$ is marked in the upper-right region of the intersection with line $r$ (above the line, to the right of $n$). The angle $x°$ is marked in the lower-left region of the intersection with line $s$ (below the line, to the left of $n$). Since these angles are on opposite sides of the transversal and both lie exterior to (outside) the parallel lines, they are alternate exterior angles.

steps.title: Apply the alternate exterior angles theorem
steps.content: When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, alternate exterior angles are congruent. Therefore, the $162°$ angle at line $r$ equals the $x°$ angle at line $s$:$$x = 162$$
